title: Square your shit — title-as-imperative
html:
TL;DR

  • Empty-body Apple Note from October 14, 2019 — three-word title as imperative: "Square your shit." No body, no list, no plan. The order is the artifact.
  • Companion-corrective to "So much shit to do, that we do none" eight days earlier — first the diagnosis, then the command.
  • Square (verb) = settle accounts, align corners, make right — military / blue-collar register that recurs in Yahir's max effort commit vocabulary years later.
  • Sits in the dense October-2019 GP teen era cluster of brevity-notes (one-line dream-logs, three-word manifests) — the period when Yahir's is weighted toward self-coaching aphorisms over to-do lists.
  • Maps to the later no runway discipline / get-your-shit-together postures across the Austin thesis era — same speaker-to-self register, six years apart.

Note

Three words. The note is the order; the order is the note. Yahir at 16 issuing himself the imperative he just named the absence of in the previous week's note. There's no plan because the plan would be the avoiding — naming the verb is the action.

The word square matters. It's geometric (corners aligned), financial (accounts settled), military (parade-ground tidy), and slang (right with someone). Teen Yahir picks the verb that does all four jobs — a one-syllable demand on the self to make all the planes match. The same instinct that later picks commit, concentrate, don't dilute in the Austin frame.

Title-only notes are not failed notes; they're the most compressed form of the form — the title saved into a is a surfaceable order to a future self.

Connections

  • 2019 10 06 so much shit to do that we do none — the diagnosis
  • — the form
  • max effort commit — the verb's adult version
  • no runway discipline — the Austin-era recurrence
  • — the storage form
